source.title: Cellulitis and erysipelas: antimicrobial prescribing
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults, young people, and children with cellulitis or erysipelas.
source.setting: Initial and follow-up assessment, antimicrobial review, reassessment, and referral decisions.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting cellulitis extent, site, severity, exposure and microbiology context, reassessment, worsening, severe pain, systemic illness, and clinician decisions. It does not diagnose infection, prescribe antibiotics, or generate referral.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, lack of improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, spread, underlying conditions, serious alternative diagnoses, and microbiology results during clinician reassessment.

items.rationale: The exact skin-infection reassessment section supports documenting interval history, change, symptoms, clinician skin inspection, distribution or extent, general appearance, secondary infection signs, severe pain, immune context and already available microbiology without asserting cellulitis, erysipelas, or treatment failure.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.

source.title: Cellulitis and erysipelas: antimicrobial prescribing
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults, young people, and children with cellulitis or erysipelas.
source.setting: Initial and follow-up assessment, antimicrobial review, reassessment, and referral decisions.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting cellulitis extent, site, severity, exposure and microbiology context, reassessment, worsening, severe pain, systemic illness, and clinician decisions. It does not diagnose infection, prescribe antibiotics, or generate referral.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.13–1.1.14 — clinician referral and specialist advice criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.

items.rationale: The exact clinician-referral section supports documenting a clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up and patient questions only; it does not generate escalation.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting skin redness and swelling extent, site, severity, broken skin, penetrating or water exposure, microbiology already obtained, skin-tone visibility, and treatment already prescribed without generating diagnosis or treatment.
